title: Enable Internet Explorer (IE) mode for a site in Edge
answer:
Per-user (test only):
1. Open `edge://settings/defaultBrowser`.
2. Set 'Allow sites to be reloaded in Internet Explorer mode' to **Allow**.
3. Restart Edge.
4. Right-click the tab → 'Reload tab in Internet Explorer mode'.

Enterprise (recommended):
1. Author an IE Mode site list (XML) and host it on a URL reachable by clients.
2. Set the `InternetExplorerIntegrationSiteList` Edge policy to that URL.
3. Set `InternetExplorerIntegrationLevel` = `IEMode (1)`.
Users do not need to manually enable IE mode when the policy is in place.

title: Reset Microsoft Edge to default settings
answer:
1. Open `edge://settings/resetProfileSettings`.
2. Click **Reset**.
This resets the startup page, new tab page, search engine and pinned tabs, disables extensions, and clears temporary data (cookies, cache). It does **not** delete history, favorites, or passwords.

If reset doesn't help, create a new profile (`edge://settings/profiles`) and verify there. As a last resort, close Edge and delete the profile folder under `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Microsoft\Edge\User Data\<Profile>` after backing it up.

title: Why does Edge say 'Managed by your organization'?
answer:
Edge shows this banner whenever **any** policy is applied via:
- Group Policy (`HKLM/HKCU\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Edge`)
- An MDM (Intune)
- A force-installed extension
- Some third-party security products that write Edge policies

Go to `edge://policy` to see every applied policy and its source. To diagnose programmatically, use the `edge_policy` skill in this collection. On a personal device the cause is usually a leftover policy from an installed app — search the registry path above and remove unused values.

title: Set Microsoft Edge as the default browser
answer:
1. Windows: Settings → Apps → Default apps → search for Edge → **Set default**.
2. Or open `edge://settings/defaultBrowser` and click **Make default**.
3. To set per-file-type/protocol associations (HTTP, HTTPS, .htm, .html, PDF, SVG), use the same Default apps page.

Enterprises: deploy `DefaultAssociationsConfiguration` via Group Policy (`Computer Configuration → Administrative Templates → Windows Components → File Explorer`).

title: Turn on Microsoft Edge sync
answer:
1. Open `edge://settings/profiles` and sign in with a Microsoft account (consumer) or work/school account (Entra ID).
2. Click **Sync** and toggle the categories you want (Favorites, Passwords, Settings, Open tabs, History, Extensions, Collections, Payment info).
3. For organizational accounts, the IT admin can disable categories via `SyncDisabled` or `SyncTypesListDisabled` policies — check `edge://policy`.

If sync is stuck, run `edge_diagnostics` and look for the `sync_error` problem type, or visit `edge://sync-internals`.

title: Where are Microsoft Edge experimental flags?
answer:
Open `edge://flags`. These are unsupported experiments — use only for testing. Common picks:
- `#edge-sleeping-tabs` (memory savings)
- `#parallel-downloading`
- `#edge-tab-groups-collapse-freezing`

To persist flags via launch parameters, edit the Edge shortcut Target and append `--enable-features=Feature1,Feature2`.

title: Clear Edge cache, cookies, or browsing history
answer:
Press **Ctrl+Shift+Del** (or open `edge://settings/clearBrowserData`). Choose:
- Time range (Last hour … All time)
- Items: Browsing history, Download history, Cookies and other site data, Cached images and files, Passwords, Autofill form data, Site permissions.

Click **Clear now**. To auto-clear on close, configure `edge://settings/privacy` → 'Choose what to clear every time you close the browser'.

title: Open an InPrivate (incognito) window
answer:
Keyboard: **Ctrl+Shift+N**. Or click the profile menu → 'New InPrivate window'.

InPrivate keeps no history, cookies, or form data after the window closes. It does **not** hide your activity from your employer, ISP, or the sites you visit. Extensions are disabled by default; enable per-extension via `edge://extensions` → 'Allow in InPrivate'.

title: Most useful Microsoft Edge keyboard shortcuts
answer:
| Action | Shortcut |
|---|---|
| New tab | Ctrl+T |
| Reopen closed tab | Ctrl+Shift+T |
| Switch tabs | Ctrl+Tab / Ctrl+Shift+Tab |
| Tab N (1..8) | Ctrl+N |
| Last tab | Ctrl+9 |
| Address bar | Ctrl+L or Alt+D or F6 |
| Find on page | Ctrl+F |
| DevTools | F12 or Ctrl+Shift+I |
| Browser Task Manager | Shift+Esc |
| Zoom in/out/reset | Ctrl++ / Ctrl+- / Ctrl+0 |
| Clear browsing data | Ctrl+Shift+Del |
| Print | Ctrl+P |
| Full screen | F11 |
| InPrivate | Ctrl+Shift+N |

title: Open Edge's built-in Browser Task Manager
answer: Press **Shift+Esc** inside Edge (or Menu → More tools → Browser task manager). You'll see per-tab / per-extension / per-process CPU, memory, network, and process IDs. Right-click a column header to add hidden columns like 'Process priority' or 'Idle wake-ups'.

title: Enable Sleeping Tabs to save memory
answer:
Open `edge://settings/system`. Under **Optimize Performance**:
1. Toggle **Save resources with sleeping tabs** on.
2. Set 'Put inactive tabs to sleep after' (default 2h, can drop to 30s).
3. Add exceptions for sites that should never sleep.

This can cut Edge's working set 30–80% on long-running sessions.

title: Create or switch Microsoft Edge profiles
answer:
Click the profile icon (top right) → **Add profile** → choose Work or Personal → optionally sign in.

Each profile has its own bookmarks, history, extensions, and cookies. Files on disk live at `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Microsoft\Edge\User Data\<Profile name>`. To open a specific profile from a shortcut, append `--profile-directory="Profile 1"` to the Target of an Edge shortcut.

title: Import bookmarks/passwords/history from another browser
answer: Open `edge://settings/importData`. Choose source (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge Legacy, HTML file, CSV). Tick the data types and click **Import**. Note: importing passwords from Chrome requires Chrome to be installed and the source profile to be signed in.

title: Uninstall Microsoft Edge
answer:
On Windows 10/11, the Edge Stable channel is treated as a system component and the standard 'Uninstall' option in Settings → Apps may be greyed out.

Supported removals:
- **Edge Beta / Dev / Canary**: Settings → Apps → Installed apps → Uninstall normally.
- **Edge Stable**: not officially uninstallable on consumer Windows 11; on Windows 10 N or specific EEA SKUs the option is exposed. Group Policy `Allowsxs` / `RemoveAppx` paths exist for managed devices.

If the user just wants Edge to stop launching, change file/protocol associations to another browser instead.

title: Update Microsoft Edge to the latest version
answer:
Open `edge://settings/help`. Edge will check Microsoft Update and download/install any newer build, then prompt to restart. Auto-updates happen in the background via the **Microsoft Edge Update** service (`MicrosoftEdgeUpdate.exe`).

If updates are blocked, look for `UpdateDefault`, `Update{56eb18f8-...}`, or `Install{56eb18f8-...}` under `HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\EdgeUpdate` (or run the `edge_policy` skill).

title: Open Microsoft Edge DevTools
answer:
Press **F12** or **Ctrl+Shift+I**. Right-click any element → **Inspect** to jump to the Elements panel for that node.

Useful entry points:
- `Ctrl+Shift+C` — pick mode (hover → inspect)
- `Ctrl+Shift+J` — go straight to Console
- `Ctrl+Shift+M` — toggle device emulation
- `Ctrl+Shift+P` — DevTools command palette
